questions.question: What is the key constraint of Flux unidirectional data flow?
options: State must be stored in a single global object
The view never modifies state directly — it always goes through an action
Actions must be synchronous to maintain traceability

questions.question: Why did Pinia drop mutations compared to Vuex?
options: Mutations caused performance issues in large applications
TypeScript doesn't support the mutation pattern
The extra indirection wasn't worth the boilerplate
Mutations were incompatible with the Composition API

questions.question: What does Vue's v-model actually expand to under the hood?
options: A reactive proxy that writes directly to component state
A :value binding plus an @input event that updates the source
A two-way reactive binding using computed getters and setters
A watcher that syncs the DOM value with the data model
